name: Veterinary Records & Controlled Drugs (AU)
description: Reference policy aligned with AVA professional conduct expectations and state poisons schedules. Adjust retention by jurisdiction.

data.text: Records & controlled drugs — AVA / state aligned
Applies to every clinical encounter recorded by veterinarians and registered nurses in this practice.
Records must be detailed enough that another veterinarian can continue care without reinterpretation.
Patient records are retained for at least 7 years from the date of the last consultation, or longer where the relevant state requires.
Informed consent is documented in writing for invasive procedures, anaesthesia, euthanasia, and off-label drug use.
Schedule 4 and Schedule 8 controlled drugs are entered into the controlled drugs register per the state poisons schedule, with every transaction witnessed and reconciled at least monthly.
Each entry identifies the signing veterinarian; corrections are made by addendum, never by overwrite.

clauses.title: Records sufficient for handover
clauses.body: You must keep clinical records detailed enough for another veterinarian to continue care without reinterpretation.

clauses.title: Minimum 7-year retention
clauses.body: You must retain patient records for at least 7 years from the last consultation, or longer if state law requires.

clauses.title: Written consent for invasive care
clauses.body: You must document informed consent in writing for invasive procedures, anaesthesia, euthanasia, and off-label drug use.

clauses.title: Controlled drugs register kept
clauses.body: You must record S4 / S8 transactions in the controlled drugs register per the state poisons schedule and reconcile monthly with a witness.

clauses.title: Veterinarian sign-off identified
clauses.body: Each record entry must identify the signing veterinarian; corrections are by addendum only.
